Introduction

Tanzania600K —
A Nation-Changing Project

AM Mega Housing Solutions Limited (AMMHSO) is a Tanzanian company incorporated under the Companies Ordinance Cap. 212 (R.E.) 2002 of the Laws of Tanzania. Operating from its headquarters in Dodoma, AMMHSO leads the most ambitious affordable housing initiative in Tanzania's history: the Tanzania600K Project — the development of 600,000 housing units with full infrastructure and social amenities across nine major Tanzanian cities.

The project is structured into 144 integrated sustainable human settlement packages, each comprising 4,000 homes across low, middle, and high-income categories, and supporting approximately 20,000 residents. Every package includes schools, hospitals, shopping centres, employment areas, parks, and places of worship — a complete community, not merely a housing estate.

Implemented over 15 years in three consecutive five-year phases, the Tanzania600K Project addresses Tanzania's housing deficit of over 3 million units while generating significant employment, stimulating local enterprise, and contributing to government revenue through PAYE, VAT, and corporate taxes. AMMHSO holds a 27% equity stake, with 70% sourced from strategic investors and 3% of post-tax profits allocated to host city councils.

Registration: AM Mega Housing Solution Ltd — Company Ordinance Cap. 212 (R.E.) 2002
Reg. No.: 140359033 — Principal Office: Plot No. 104, Upanga, Magole Street, Anverton Park, Dar Es Salaam — Email: nkanjuni2022@gmail.com / fatou.mwinyi@gmail.com

Flagship Initiative

The Tanzania600K Project

600,000 affordable housing units. 9 major cities. 144 integrated settlement packages. 15 years. USD 107.7 billion total investment. This is not simply a construction project. It is Tanzania’s most ambitious programme of community building since independence.

6 packages × 4,000 units. Avg net profit margin: 51.3%. Recouping period: 5 years.

Housing Typologies

ASMA (block of 12 flats) • XENIA (2, 3 & 4-bed progression bungalows) • RUZUNA (row bungalows) • NIKE (7-flat block + penthouse) • RIZIKI (22-flat mid-rise) • RIZIKI II (70-flat high-income block with penthouses) • CAMILIA (3-bed detached) • ARIELLA (4-bed row house) • SORAIYAH (4-bed detached duplex) • 2-bed Bungalows • Row Houses • Duplexes

Purpose

Mission & Vision

◆

Our Mission

To transcend boundaries, pioneer change, and leave a lasting positive impact across Africa. We meticulously plan, execute seamlessly, and commit to excellence — turning the challenges of inadequate housing into sustainable opportunities for brighter futures.

Aligned with the Global and National UN Habitat Agendas, we cater to community needs, advance socio-economic progress, and empower individuals through world-class, reliable, and affordable housing services. With innovation and integrity, we create a future where housing empowers transformation.

◇

Our Vision

To be the foremost and most trusted source for diverse land-related investments and housing services — not only within Tanzania, but across neighbouring states and the broader African continent.

We aspire to provide adequate shelter for all, drive urban renewal, support sustainable development, and contribute to poverty alleviation through industrialisation and the creation of substantial employment opportunities for present and future generations.

What We Stand For

Core Values & Culture

AMMHSO is dedicated to achieving what conventional thinking considers unattainable. We transform societal challenges into sustainable business concepts that serve fundamental human needs.

Integrity

Honesty in every approach — in communication with clients, partners, governments, and communities. We do not make promises we cannot keep, and we account for our actions openly.

Excellence

A constant drive to improve the quality of services to world-class standards. We refuse to accept mediocrity in design, construction, management, or client relations.

Speed

Delivering services when and where required. Through advanced construction technologies, we reduce project timelines by up to 40% compared to traditional methods.

Inclusivity

Prioritising the needs of those previously excluded from property markets — low- and middle-income households who deserve safe, dignified, and affordable housing.

Innovation

Combining traditional craftsmanship with cutting-edge building systems, BIM technology, and international partnerships to find smarter, cheaper, and faster housing solutions.

Community Impact

Our business exists to serve communities — not the preferences of a select few. Poverty reduction through industrialisation and job creation is at the core of our model.

Leadership

Meet Our Team

AMMHSO is led by a multi-disciplinary leadership team combining expertise in engineering, architecture, finance, and international development.

MEng Geomatics (New Brunswick, Canada). Member: IST, OLS (Ontario), RICS, NCPS, and Zanzibar Land Surveyors (ZLSB). Extensive practice in Tanzania and Canada.

Financial Advisor

PhD Sciences, Université Paul Sabatier, Toulouse. Fluid mechanics and petroleum expert. MD of ATGLS LLC. Formerly ENI Milan; AIG Group USA. Specialist in West & East African project financing.

What We Do

Scope of Services

AMMHSO's vertically integrated model spans the full spectrum of property development — from land acquisition and architectural design to construction, handover, and property management.

Architecture & Design

Feasibility studies, preliminary designs, working drawings, tender documentation, project supervision, and management for residential and public buildings domestically and internationally.

Affordable Housing

Designing and constructing affordable housing units, high-income residential schemes, and luxury villas. Currently delivering 65,000 Affordable Housing Units in Dodoma and Zanzibar.

Public Buildings

Design and construction of office complexes, shopping centres, healthcare facilities, educational institutions, and recreational facilities — including diplomatic missions internationally.

Integrated Community Amenities

Each 4,000-unit settlement package includes schools, hospitals (300-bed), health centres, shopping malls, employment centres, farmers’ markets, parks, places of worship, police stations, and fire stations.

GIS & Land Surveying

Cadastral base-mapping, GPS surveys, topographic surveys, legal surveys, polygon integrity checking, UTM coordinate mapping, real-time DGPS navigation, and kinematic topographic surveys.

Infrastructure & Urban Renewal

Roads, pavements, electricity, water supply, alternative energy systems, storm water drainage, and central sewerage. Urban renewal collaboration with the National Housing Corporation on Dar es Salaam high-rise and informal settlement upgrading.

How We Build

Technology & Innovation

AMMHSO partners with proven international building systems to deliver structures that are faster, cheaper, more energy-efficient, and more durable than those produced by conventional methods.

01

BIM — Building Information Modelling

Seamlessly connects design with the supply chain, streamlining project execution and cost-effectiveness whilst minimising capital tied up in lengthy construction processes.

02

REP® System — OneCo, Italy / GSM

A highly industrialised, flexible building system combining structural and additional elements. Suitable for single-family homes to high-rise structures, with maximum architectural flexibility and a 40% reduction in construction time.

03

ARGISOL® Building System

25 years of proven performance. Innovative Neopor® shuttering elements based on BASF SE technology. Energy-efficient construction adaptable to any floor plan, assembled with minimum skilled labour.

04

Western Forms — Cast-in-Place Concrete

Over 60 years of US industry leadership with 30 patents. PinLock™ Attached Hardware system ensures labour efficiency. Reduces construction costs by over 30% and accelerates project cycles by up to 50%.

Vertically Integrated Construction Model

AMMHSO's model covers the full construction spectrum: from architectural ideation and interior design, through engineering, material supply, and manufacturing of houses using pre-cast and cast-in-place concrete, to final construction and handover. This integration eliminates costly handoffs and ensures consistent quality at every stage.

40% Faster than
Traditional Build
30% Cost Reduction
Western Forms
A+ Energy Efficiency
Rating
Market Presence

The Tanzania Housing Crisis
and the Tanzania600K Response

Tanzania's housing deficit stands at over 3 million units, growing by approximately 200,000 units each year. The 2017 relocation of national government functions from Dar es Salaam to Dodoma intensified demand in the capital, whilst rapid urbanisation, internal migration, and expansion of higher education institutions continue to drive housing shortages across all major cities.

AMMHSO's Tanzania600K Project targets nine cities simultaneously: Dodoma, Dar es Salaam, Mwanza, Arusha, Morogoro, Mbeya, Tanga, Zanzibar, and Kigoma. Land has already been secured or is under acquisition in each city, with initial sites confirmed at Mapinduzi and Ihumwa (Dodoma), Matemwe (Zanzibar), and urban renewal zones in Dar es Salaam.

Target Market: Public sector employees relocating to Dodoma, private sector workers, the growing middle class, institutional buyers, and low-income households. Mortgage partnerships with CRDB Bank, NMB, Stanbic, and Equity Bank ensure accessible financing for eligible buyers.

Looking Ahead

Future Plans

Tanzania600K:
Building a Nation,
One Home at a Time

Phase I — 2026–2030

20,000 units per city across 9 cities (180,000 units total). Five packages of 4,000 units each, per city. Phase I investment: USD 3.814 billion per city. Land secured. Construction commencing in Dodoma, Zanzibar, and Dar es Salaam. Average net profit margin: 47.7%.

Phase II — 2031–2035

21,000 units per city (189,000 units total). Scaled up from Phase I learnings. Phase II investment: USD 3.870 billion per city. Full deployment across all nine cities simultaneously. Average net profit margin: 50.6%.

Phase III — 2036–2040

24,000 units per city (216,000 units total). The most profitable phase by scale. Phase III investment: USD 4.417 billion per city. Completion of all 600,000 units with full amenities. Average net profit margin: 51.3%.

Nine Target Cities

Dodoma • Dar es Salaam • Mwanza • Arusha • Morogoro • Mbeya • Tanga • Zanzibar • Kigoma. Each city receives a complete programme of housing, infrastructure, schools, hospitals, employment centres, and green parks.

Mortgage & Finance Institution

AMMHSO investors are establishing a dedicated mortgage institution to provide affordable financing rates to home buyers. Partnerships already active with CRDB, NMB, Stanbic Bank, and Equity Bank. A win-win-win for buyers, banks, and AMMHSO.

Employment & Legacy Impact

Over 425 core staff (local and international) plus thousands of skilled, semi-skilled, and unskilled construction workers across all sites. The project will generate PAYE, VAT, and corporate tax revenues, and deliver a multiplier effect through local supply chains and small enterprise growth.
